name: Goygol
description: Goygol is a charming city near the stunning Lake Goygol, one of Azerbaijan's most beautiful natural sites. The city is also known for its winemaking history and the famous Goygol Winery, established in 1860.

content.intro: Goygol is a postcard-perfect destination centered around a crystal-clear lake surrounded by lush mountains. The city's German colonial heritage adds a unique architectural flavor, and the local winery produces some of Azerbaijan's finest wines.

attractions.name: Lake Goygol
attractions.description: A stunning alpine lake formed by an earthquake, with crystal-clear waters reflecting the surrounding mountains.

attractions.name: Goygol Winery
attractions.description: One of the oldest wineries in the Caucasus, producing wines from local grape varieties since 1860.

attractions.name: Helenendorf
attractions.description: The former German colony (now Khanlar) features well-preserved 19th-century architecture.

whereToEat.name: Goygol Winery Restaurant
whereToEat.description: Wine pairing with traditional Azerbaijani dishes.

whereToEat.name: Lake View Cafe
whereToEat.description: Casual dining with views of Lake Goygol.

tips: Visit the winery for a tasting session.
Hike around Lake Goygol for the best views.
touristMistakes: Ignoring the weather—mountain climate can change quickly.
hiddenGems: The old cemetery with German tombstones.
content.howToGetThere: From Baku: 380 km. Fly to Ganja (1h) then drive 30min to Goygol.
